summary: Backend: 16/16 pytest passed (added 2 new tests covering /api/seed-demo, /api/cruxes, /api/beliefs/{id}/ripple). Frontend: All 12 iteration-2 acceptance items verified — 4 tabs, [demo] button seeds, empty-state seed buttons (ledger+graph), ExtractionResult panel with contradiction-banner FIRST + side-by-side confidence bars, ripple-panel with downstream beliefs, CRUX tab auto-fetches /api/cruxes and renders crux-item-0..N with assumption/falsifier importance, close-extraction-btn present. Visual contradiction flow verified end-to-end with the two prompts in the spec.

minor.issue: Seeds only ~1 belief out of 4 entries (response: {seeded_beliefs:1, entries:4}). Root cause: llm_service.extract_beliefs silently falls back to empty list on JSON parse failure (already flagged in iteration_1 code review) and 3 of the 4 demo prompts return 0 extractions. Endpoint returns 200, but the demo experience is degraded — the spec asks for 4 beliefs with >=1 contradiction. UI ends up showing 1-2 beliefs after seeding rather than 4.
When LLM JSON parsing fails, endpoint silently returns {count:0,results:[]} with status 200. Should surface a 502 or include a parse_warning flag so UI can retry/notify.

integration_issues.issue: compute-crux-inline-{i} button does not transition to inline cruxes view within ~20s after click in our run. The CRUX tab (same backend endpoint) works in ~5-10s. Likely either (a) LLM call took >20s in this session, or (b) network failure left cruxByBelief unset (runCrux has try/finally but no catch — a failed POST leaves the button visible without surfacing the error). Recommend adding .catch with toast.error.

action_items: Fix llm_service JSON-parse silent fallback so /api/seed-demo reliably seeds all 4 demo entries (currently 1/4). Either harden the JSON extraction (retry with stricter prompt / use json mode) or raise 502 on parse failure inside extract_beliefs so seed-demo can retry.
Add .catch to ExtractionResult.runCrux to surface failures via toast and to fall back to empty cruxes so the button hides.
Optional: tighten /api/cruxes response when an item has cruxes=[] — return an explanatory note so UI can render a hint instead of 'No cruxes computed yet.'
critical_code_review_comments: server.py seed_demo() relies on best-effort LLM extraction; with the current parser fallback this silently undermines the whole 'instant value' goal of iteration 2 — the highest-impact fix.
ExtractionResult.runCrux: missing .catch handler — a failed POST leaves the button stuck in 'thinking…' / visible state with no user feedback.
Dashboard.handleReset still uses window.confirm — works but doesn't match the terminal aesthetic and breaks playwright runs unless dialog handler is registered first.

/api/cruxes: blocking LLM calls inside an HTTP request mean cold-cache p95 can exceed 60s for limit=5. Consider returning cached cruxes immediately and computing fresh ones in the background, or stream/progressive responses.
Belief 'source_entry_id' is persisted in beliefs collection but not in the Belief Pydantic model — schema drift (also flagged in iteration_1).
_centrality_map() runs on every /beliefs, /graph, /cruxes call; for larger ledgers consider caching.

success_rate.frontend: 11/12 acceptance items fully verified; 1 item (inline crux click → cruxes render within timeout) flaky

seed_data_creation: test_zz_seed_demo_and_cruxes_and_ripple wipes DB via /api/seed-demo and exercises /api/cruxes + /api/beliefs/{id}/ripple. Ordered last (zz_ prefix) so it doesn't disturb the deterministic earlier tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_2 acceptance is broadly passing. Two follow-ups: (1) /api/seed-demo currently only persists 1 belief out of 4 due to LLM JSON parse fallback — once main agent fixes llm_service, re-run test_zz_seed_demo_and_cruxes_and_ripple and tighten the asserts (>=3 beliefs, contradiction link in graph). (2) ExtractionResult inline crux button could use a .catch for resilience. The frontend playwright run already accepts dialogs and uses force=True clicks — see /app/.screenshots/extraction.png and crux.png for visual confirmation of contradiction-banner + side-by-side confidence + ripple-panel + CRUX view.
rca of the issue: Seed-demo gap RCA: POST /api/seed-demo iterates over 4 DEMO_ENTRIES and calls create_entry(). create_entry() calls llm_service.extract_beliefs(); when the LLM returns malformed JSON, the parser in llm_service silently returns [] (already flagged in iteration_1 code review). 3 of 4 demo entries currently hit that path, so the seeded ledger only contains 1 belief. Reproduction: curl -X POST $BACKEND_URL/api/seed-demo → {"seeded_beliefs":1,"entries":4}. Mitigation options: (a) tighten the LLM prompt + use response_format json_object, (b) retry on empty extraction, (c) raise 502 from extract_beliefs on parse failure so seed-demo can fall through to a deterministic fallback.
